title: Metals Miner Lumina Ends Trading Day Flat After Toronto IPO
summary: Lumina Metals Corp. shares ended the trading day right where it started after the Canadian company and one of its shareholders raised C$406.2 million ($297 million) in an initial public offering.

title: Novo-Backed Drugmaker Avalyn Soars 64% After Trading Debut
summary: Avalyn Pharma Inc. shares jumped 64% in its trading debut, after the biopharmaceutical company raised $300 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

title: FEMA Reinstates Staff Who Criticized Trump Cuts to Agency
summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency this week told more than a dozen staffers to return to work after placing them on administrative leave eight months ago for signing an open letter criticizing the Trump administration’s cuts to disaster response, according to people familiar with the decision.

title: Thomson Family to See $1 Billion Payout as AI Hits Reuters Stock

title: US Apartment Giants AvalonBay, Equity Residential Explore $50 Billion Combination
summary: Potential mega REIT tie-up raises plenty of antitrust and political questions.

title: Alberta Energy Minister Believes New Pipeline Will Have Indigenous Support
summary: The Alberta government believes it will have Indigenous support for its plan to build a new oil pipeline to the Pacific coast when it’s unveiled in June.

title: US Critical Mineral Inventory Plan Includes Buying China Metals
summary: The US Export-Import Bank’s proposed stockpiling initiative would initially source critical minerals from anywhere in the world, including China, an official involved in the project has revealed.

title: S&P 500 Index Futures Rise on Earnings, Yen Gains: Markets Wrap
summary: S&P 500 Index futures rose in early trading, signaling that a rally which pushed Wall Street gauges to record highs on strong megacap tech earnings may have further to run. The yen gained on a report of Japan’s intervention.

title: Gold Steadies as Dollar Weakens After Japan’s Yen Intervention
summary: Gold held a gain after the US dollar retreated sharply on Japan’s intervention in the foreign-exchange market, helping to offset heightened inflation risks arising from the Iran war.

title: Goldman’s Freund Sees Early Signs of Australia M&A Rebound
summary: Australia’s subdued deals landscape is showing early signs of recovery after lagging Asia and the US, according to Goldman Sachs’ local head of mergers and acquisitions.

title: Petrobras Output Hits Record as Iran War Jolts Oil Markets
summary: Petrobras pumped record oil and gas volumes in the first quarter and ran its refineries near full capacity, underscoring Brazil’s growing importance to global energy markets disrupted by the Iran war.

title: UK Man Agrees to Plead Guilty in ‘Singapore Solution’ Tax Case
summary: A UK citizen has agreed to plead guilty over his role in helping Americans use offshore accounts to evade taxes through a scheme known as the “Singapore Solution,” US prosecutors said.

title: Oil Holds Weekly Gain as Trump Says Iran Blockade Is Working
summary: Oil held its second weekly gain as US President Donald Trump said he was sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports, elevating concerns the vital Strait of Hormuz would not reopen anytime soon.

title: Tanger Beats Earnings Estimates as Gen Z Drives Return to Physical Stores
summary: Tanger Inc. posted first-quarter earnings that beat expectations and bumped up its full-year guidance, bolstered by robust tenant occupancy rates and a return to physical retail stores by Gen Z shoppers.

title: First Brands Creditor Sues Auditor BDO Over Missed Red Flags
summary: A lender that owns First Brands Corp. debt sued auditor BDO USA P.C., alleging it failed to detect problems at the auto parts supplier before it collapsed into bankruptcy and federal prosecutors accused founder Patrick James of fraud.

title: Air Canada Pulls 2026 Outlook on Rising Jet Fuel Costs
summary: Air Canada suspended its full-year 2026 financial guidance because of the surge in jet fuel prices caused by the war in Iran.

title: Emerging Stocks See Best Month Since 2022 Amid AI Boom, Oil Fear
summary: Emerging-market equities posted the best month since 2022, fueled by rallies in Asian technology shares on optimism over artificial intelligence demand, even as oil supply shock persists amid a US-Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: The Business of Influence
summary: Kyle Cooke, Founder, Loverboy and Jason Tartick, Co-Founder, Rewired Talent Management; Host, Trading Secrets discuss turning television and social media visibility into scalable businesses and long-term value with Bloomberg’s Meg Szabo at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trader Mercuria Sues Baltic Exchange Over Oil Shipping Benchmark
summary: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. is suing the Baltic Exchange over the alleged distortion of a key benchmark for the cost of shipping oil from the Middle East.

title: Yen Soars as Japan Intervenes After Issuing ‘Final’ Warning
summary: The yen surged more than 2%, for its biggest gain in three years, after Japan intervened in the foreign-exchange market hours after officials delivered a “final” warning to investors against selling the currency.

title: AI Debt Investors Show Fatigue After $300 Billion Binge
summary: After a $300 billion AI debt binge that spanned every corner of the credit market, investors are starting to show some signs of fatigue.

title: Bloomberg Markets 4/30/2026
summary: "Bloomberg Markets" follows the market moves across every global asset class and discusses the biggest issues for Wall Street. Today's guests: Advisors Capital Management Portfolio Manager & Partner JoAnne Feeney, Hyatt Hotels Chairman President & CEO Mark Hoplamazian, Bokeh Capital Founder Kim Forrest, Royal Caribbean CEO Jason Liberty (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Alphabet Has the Second Biggest One-Day Jump in Market Cap Ever
summary: Alphabet Inc. added a historic amount to its market capitalization on Thursday, following blowout quarterly earnings that sent the stock skyrocketing.

title: Stocks Hit Record at End of Best Month Since 2020: Markets Wrap
summary: Signs of strength in the world’s largest economy fueled investor optimism about the outlook for Corporate America’s earnings, driving stocks to their best month since late 2020.

title: Banamex Sells Global Bonds for First Time Since Citi Sale
summary: Mexico’s Grupo Financiero Banamex has tapped global markets for the first time since Citigroup Inc. started offloading its stake in the bank.

title: Gold Rebounds as Speculation of Yen Intervention Weakens Dollar
summary: Gold bounced back from a three-day decline as the dollar sank and traders weighed the prospect of a fresh US military engagement in Iran.

title: Meta Sells $25 Billion of Debt as Investor Worry, Fatigue Builds
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds, hitting the market with a jumbo deal for the second time in six months as investors are starting to show some signs of fatigue.

title: World Cup Executives on Scaling a Global Event
summary: Amy Hopfinger, Chief Business & Strategy Officer, FIFA World Cup 2026 and Alex Lasry, CEO, NYNJ Host Committee, FIFA World Cup 2026 discuss scaling a global tournament and economic impact with Bloomberg’s Vanessa Perdomo at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Alphabet Soars, S&P Hits Another Record | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wayfair CFO Sees Growth Despite 'Challenged' Consumer
summary: Wayfair CFO Kate Gulliver said despite an earnings miss by the furniture seller the company feels 'great' about the growth outlook. Gulliver said Wayfiar is less focused on when 'challenged' consumers will recover, but the strength of internal initiatives that she believes will drive growth.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Susie Wolff on What's Next for F1
summary: Susie Wolff, Managing Director, F1 Academy discusses leadership, talent development and her racing career journey with Bloomberg’s Hannah Elliott and Matt Miller at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dollar Wraps Worst Month Since June as Haven Trade Unwinds
summary: The US dollar wrapped up its worst month since June as the prospect of peace talks to end the Iran war led traders to unravel bets on the haven currency.

title: Inside Cadillac's New Formula 1 Team
summary: Dan Towriss, CEO, TWG Motorsports discusses launching Cadillac’s Formula 1 team and the role of institutional capital in global motorsport with Bloomberg's Hannah Elliott at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AIG Profit Jumps as CEO Zaffino’s Strategic Deals Take Effect
summary: American International Group Inc. reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, driven in part by a jump in net premiums written and lower catastrophe losses.
